perturbation.original_text: (b) IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE TO THE OTHER PARTY, IN CONTRACT, IN TORT OR OTHERWISE, FOR ANY INDIRECT, SPECIAL, INCIDENTAL, PUNITIVE OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES OF ANY NATURE OR KIND RELATING TO OR IN CONNECTION WITH SUCH PARTY’S PERFORMANCE OR NON -PERFORMANCE OF ITS OBLIGATIONS UNDER THIS AGREEMENT .
perturbation.changed_text: (b) IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE TO THE OTHER PARTY, IN CONTRACT, IN TORT OR OTHERWISE, FOR ANY INDIRECT, SPECIAL, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES OF ANY NATURE OR KIND RELATING TO OR IN CONNECTION WITH SUCH PARTY’S PERFORMANCE OR NON -PERFORMANCE OF ITS OBLIGATIONS UNDER THIS AGREEMENT. Liability for incidental or punitive damages is capped at $100.
perturbation.explanation: By limiting the exclusion of damages, while also capping incidental or punitive damages, the original broad exclusion is contradicted, creating uncertainty about the types of recoverable damages and their limits, which is not in alignment with common law interpretations.
perturbation.law_explanation: UCC 2-719(3) states that consequential damages may be limited or excluded unless the limitation or exclusion is unconscionable. The modified text both limits and allows for possible incidental and punitive damages, but the standard for conscionability could be questioned due to the very low cap of $100. The UCC governs contracts like this one.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-719. Contractual Modification or Limitation of Remedy. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-719. Contractual Modification or Limitation of Remedy. (1)Subject to the provisions of subsections (2) and (3) of this section and of the preceding section on liquidation and limitation of damages, (a) the agreement may provide for remedies in addition to or in substitution for those provided in this Article and may limit or alter the measure of damages recoverable under this Article, as by limiting the buyer's remedies to return of the goods and repayment of the price or to repair and replacement of non-conforming goods or parts; and (b) resort to a remedy as provided is optional unless the remedy is expressly agreed to be exclusive, in which case it is the sole remedy. (2)Where circumstances cause an exclusive or limited remedy to fail of its essential purpose, remedy may be had as provided in this Act. (3) Consequential damages may be limited or excluded unless the limitation or exclusion is unconscionable. Limitation of consequential damages for injury to the person in the case of consumer goods is prima facie unconscionable but limitation of damages where the loss is commercial is not. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes

perturbation.justification: The original text excludes liability for incidental and punitive damages. The changed text allows liability for incidental and punitive damages, capping it at $100. This is a Misaligned Terminology Legal Contradiction because, while UCC 2-719(3) allows for the limitation or exclusion of consequential damages, the changed text contradicts the original intent of excluding these damages entirely. The law snippet states "Consequential damages may be limited or excluded unless the limitation or exclusion is unconscionable." The modification introduces the possibility of such damages, directly opposing the initial exclusion.

perturbation.original_text: (d) HAVING HAD AN OPPORTUNITY TO CONSULT WITH COUNSEL, THE PARTIES HEREBY KNOWINGLY, VOLUNTARILY AND TO THE FULL EST EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW WAIVE ALL RIGHTS TO TRIAL BY JURY AND AGREE THAT ANY AND ALL MATTERS SHALL BE DEC IDED BY A JUDGE .
perturbation.changed_text: (d) THE PARTIES HEREBY WAIVE ALL RIGHTS TO TRIAL BY JURY AND AGREE THAT ANY AND ALL MATTERS SHALL BE DECIDED BY A JUDGE.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the explicit statement that the parties had the 'opportunity to consult with counsel' and that the waiver is 'knowingly, voluntarily and to the full extent permitted by law,' the altered clause undermines the enforceability of the jury trial waiver, potentially conflicting with constitutional requirements for such waivers.
perturbation.law_explanation: The 7th Amendment guarantees the right to a jury trial in civil cases. While this right can be waived, the waiver must be knowing and voluntary. Removing the explicit acknowledgment of the opportunity to consult counsel weakens the argument that the waiver was truly voluntary, potentially rendering the waiver unenforceable under the 7th Amendment and applicable case law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Seventh Amendment | U.S. Constitution | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Seventh Amendment The Seventh Amendment continues a practice from English common law of distinguishing civil claims which must be tried before a jury (absent waiver by the parties) from claims and issues that may be heard by a judge alone. It only governs federal civil courts and has no application to civil courts set up by the states when those courts are hearing only disputes of state law . Learn more... Primary tabs Amendment VII In suits at common law, where the value in controversy shall exceed twenty dollars, the right of trial by jury shall be preserved, and no fact tried by a jury, shall be otherwise reexamined in any court of the United States, than according to the rules of the common law. Wex Resources Criminal Law Criminal Procedure Jury Other Resources US Code: Title 18 Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure ‹ Sixth Amendment up Eighth Amendment › U.S. Constitution Toolbox Explanation of the Constitution - from the Congressional Research Service
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the explicit acknowledgement that the parties had the opportunity to consult with counsel and that the waiver is knowing and voluntary. The scraped snippet from the Seventh Amendment states that the right to a jury trial can be waived. However, the Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation highlight that such a waiver must be knowing and voluntary. Removing the explicit acknowledgement weakens the argument that the waiver was truly voluntary, potentially rendering the waiver unenforceable. The original text included language ensuring it was 'knowingly, voluntarily and to the full extent permitted by law' which the altered text omitted.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement is and shall remain the property of Disclo sing Party. Receiving Party shall not alter or remove any confidentiality or proprietary marking on the Confidential Information and , subject to the forego- ing, may make such limited number of copies of Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to the ex tent necessary to achieve the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement may become the property of Receiving Party after one year from disclosure. Receiving Party may alter or remove any confidentiality or proprietary marking on the Confidential Information and may make an unlimited number of copies of Disclosing Party's Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the provision that Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party. By stating that the information may become the Receiving Party's property, and by allowing the removal of proprietary markings and unlimited copies, the core principle of confidentiality and ownership is undermined, conflicting with trade secret protections.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Illinois Trade Secrets Act protects trade secrets from misappropriation. By allowing the Receiving Party to potentially own the information, remove proprietary markings, and make unlimited copies, the altered text effectively allows for actions that would constitute misappropriation under the Act, as it undermines the reasonable efforts to maintain secrecy required for trade secret protection.

perturbation.justification: The changed text fundamentally contradicts the original agreement regarding ownership and usage of confidential information. The original text clearly states that the confidential information remains the property of the Disclosing Party, while the changed text allows the Receiving Party to potentially own it after one year. Additionally, the changed text permits the Receiving Party to alter proprietary markings and make unlimited copies, which directly undermines the protection of confidential information. The Law Explanation states that these changes would constitute misappropriation under the Illinois Trade Secrets Act as it undermines the reasonable efforts to maintain secrecy required for trade secret protection. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 is irrelevant, the overall analysis clearly indicates a legal contradiction due to the altered terms impacting the confidentiality and ownership of trade secrets.

perturbation.original_text: (c) This Agreement shall be governed by, and interpreted in accordance with, the laws of the State of Illinois without regard to pr inciples of conflicts of law.
perturbation.changed_text: (c) This Agreement shall be governed by, and interpreted in accordance with, the laws of the State of California, regardless of the parties' principal places of business or where the confidential information is disclosed.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the governing law from Illinois to California, especially when the contract specifies that one party (LiiON, LLC) has its principal place of business in Illinois, and without any clear connection to California, could conflict with principles of contract law regarding choice of law and forum selection.
perturbation.law_explanation: Restatement (Second) of Conflict of Laws § 187 addresses choice of law in contract cases. While parties can generally choose the governing law, the choice must have a reasonable relationship to the contract or the parties, or there must be a reasonable basis for the parties' choice. Choosing California law when the contract is primarily centered in Illinois, without any obvious connection to California, could be deemed unenforceable under this section, particularly if Illinois has a materially greater interest in the determination of the particular issue.

perturbation.justification: The modified text changes the governing law from Illinois to California. According to the Law Explanation, Restatement (Second) of Conflict of Laws § 187 requires that a choice of law have a reasonable relationship to the contract or the parties. Since the original contract specifies that LiiON, LLC has its principal place of business in Illinois, and the changed text doesn't establish any clear connection to California, this could be deemed unenforceable. The change therefore creates a legal contradiction by potentially violating principles of contract law.
